title: PreToolUse enforcement hooks scope on op-shape + block-name, never on the target substrate — they fire on writes to throwaway/non-active substrates outside the convention they protect
description: gap-register-guard.sh matches on write-op name (append/update/upsert-block-item) AND planning-block name/array-key, and block-pi-context-glue.sh matches on the pi-context CLI invocation shape; neither inspects --cwd or the active-substrate pointer (.pi-context.json contextDir). A planning-block write or a CLI invocation whose --cwd targets a throwaway/non-active substrate is blocked identically to one against the active substrate, though the filing-provenance and direct-drive conventions the hooks enforce scope to the active substrate (filings composed verbatim downstream INTO the active substrate; direct-drive discipline against the live substrate). The hook input carries the --cwd text in tool_input.command but the hooks do not parse it. Class: enforcement hooks scoped by op-shape rather than target-substrate, firing outside the active substrate they protect — covers both installed Bash-matcher hooks (gap-register-guard.sh, block-pi-context-glue.sh). The not-installed block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh spec (analysis/2026-06-06-substrate-cli-bypass-prevention-hook-spec.md) is the resource-scoped counter-example, matching the .context* family by path.

evidence.reference: the sole match test: (write-op name) AND (planning block-name or array-key); never reads --cwd or resolves the active-substrate pointer
the confirmed sibling: matches the pi-context CLI invocation shape alone; its block tests inspect only command-string glue, never --cwd or the active substrate

the investigation: matching logic quoted, the --cwd /tmp/gapAtest block repro, the block-pi-context-glue sibling confirmation, the block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh resource-scoped counter-example, the class verdict

impact: Every runtime demo or test that must seed a planning block into a throwaway --cwd substrate, or drive the CLI against one, hits the active-substrate guards — blocking legitimate non-active-substrate work. It blocked the TASK-033 runtime-demo seed against /tmp.
proposed_resolution: Determined by the implementing pass (Experience-Gap Handling): scope the hooks to the active substrate — parse --cwd from the command string (default to the project cwd when absent), resolve .pi-context.json contextDir at that cwd, and guard only writes/invocations targeting the active substrate; or a coarser approximation (guard only when --cwd is absent or names a .context* dir). The not-installed block-substrate-cli-bypass.sh spec's resource-scoped recognizer is the available pattern. Subtlety to honor: the hook input carries the --cwd text but no structured active-substrate resolution. Grounding: analysis/2026-06-13-install-surface-and-guard-scope-gaps.md.
